understanding.goal_understanding: Advance through all KA59 levels by placing a pushable object (block) onto the goal tile(s) marked '+' in the semantic grid (or otherwise triggering the engine-reported WIN condition) within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The currently selected piece (shown as 'P' in the semantic grid) moves one cell per action, which corresponds to a 3-pixel shift in the direction pressed (x increases to the right, y increases downward). Movement can be blocked by walls ('#') or other solid obstacles; the movement_blocked flags are only hints and don’t guarantee failure. If a pushable block ('B') is directly adjacent in the move direction and the space beyond it is free, the selected piece can push it one cell. Levels may contain multiple selectables ('S'); control can be switched by issuing a CLICK action targeting that selectable’s exact pixel/grid position as listed in the state.
